name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
description: Anthropic's best speed/intelligence balance — the value workhorse for agentic and production workloads at $3/$15 per 1M tokens, with a 1M token context window, adaptive thinking, the effort parameter including 'max', and strong computer-use accuracy.

evidence.value: Recommended model for agentic coding at the Sonnet tier; successor to Claude Sonnet 4.5
Documented as the drop-in upgrade target for Sonnet 4.5, 4.0, 3.7, and 3.5 coding workloads

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Review of official model documentation and positioning for software engineering workloads

evidence.value: Adaptive thinking supported; effort defaults to high, scaling reasoning depth with task complexity

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Review of documented thinking capabilities and reasoning benchmark positioning

evidence.value: Positioned as Anthropic's best combination of speed and intelligence

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Comprehensive knowledge and multimodal capability review against official documentation

evidence.value: Effort parameter (low/medium/high/max) gives explicit, repeatable quality/cost control; strong computer-use accuracy with adaptive thinking at high effort

output_consistency.methodology: Internal testing of output stability across effort levels and adaptive thinking

evidence.value: Typical response time ~1.5s for standard prompts at low/medium effort; faster than Opus tier

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency for API requests with standard prompt sizes

evidence.value: p95 latency ~3.5s; higher at high/max effort with adaptive thinking

latency_p95.methodology: 95th percentile response time across diverse workloads

evidence.value: 1M token context window; 64K max output tokens
99.9%+ uptime (last 90 days)

uptime.methodology: Historical uptime data from official status page

performance_reliability.notes: The value workhorse of the Claude lineup: near-Opus intelligence at Sonnet latency and price, with a 1M context window, adaptive thinking, and the full effort range including 'max'.

evidence.value: Strong resistance to prompt injection in agentic and computer-use settings

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Testing against OWASP LLM01 prompt injection attacks

evidence.value: Constitutional AI alignment with well-calibrated refusals
No training on user data without explicit consent

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of privacy policies and data handling practices

evidence.value: Released with comprehensive safety evaluations under the Responsible Scaling Policy

output_safety.methodology: Comprehensive safety testing across harmful content categories

evidence.value: API key authentication, HTTPS only, rate limiting; assistant prefills removed (400), closing a response-steering vector

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features and best practices

security.notes: Strong safety posture. Like Opus 4.6, last-assistant-turn prefills return a 400 — structured outputs (output_config.format) are the supported replacement.

evidence.value: Data residency options for US and EU customers

data_residency.methodology: Review of enterprise documentation and privacy policies

evidence.value: Opt-out available, no training on API data by default

training_data_optout.methodology: Analysis of privacy policy and data usage terms

evidence.value: API prompts and outputs not retained (except for trust & safety)

data_retention.methodology: Review of terms of service and data retention policies

evidence.value: Customer responsible for PII redaction

pii_handling.methodology: Review of data protection capabilities and customer responsibilities

evidence.value: SOC 2 Type II, GDPR compliant, HIPAA eligible

compliance_certifications.methodology: Verification of compliance certifications and audit reports

evidence.value: Ephemeral data processing, no storage of prompts/outputs

privacy_compliance.notes: Same enterprise-grade privacy posture as the Opus tier: ephemeral data handling, strong certifications, HIPAA eligible.

evidence.value: Adaptive thinking and the effort parameter make reasoning depth explicit and controllable

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning transparency and explanation capabilities

evidence.value: Improved factual calibration over Sonnet 4.5, especially with adaptive thinking enabled

hallucination_rate.methodology: Testing on factual QA datasets and real-world usage

evidence.value: Regular bias testing and mitigation

bias_fairness.methodology: Evaluation on bias benchmarks and diverse demographic testing

evidence.value: Model expresses uncertainty appropriately; adaptive thinking scales effort with problem difficulty

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment of confidence expression in outputs

evidence.value: Comprehensive model documentation with capabilities, limitations, and migration guidance from Sonnet 4.5

model_card_quality.methodology: Review of documentation completeness and clarity

evidence.value: General description provided, detailed sources not disclosed

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Constitutional AI safety guardrails with well-calibrated refusals

trust_transparency.notes: Transparent compute controls (adaptive thinking + effort) and thorough migration documentation. Follows instructions closely, reducing prompt-engineering opacity.

evidence.value: Adaptive thinking, effort parameter incl. max, structured outputs, streaming, tool use; prefills removed in favor of output_config.format

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness

evidence.value: Official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Java, Go, Ruby, C#, PHP — actively maintained

sdk_quality.methodology: Review of SDK quality, documentation, and maintenance

evidence.value: Clear versioning with advance deprecation notice; documented migration path from Sonnet 4.5 and retired 3.x Sonnets

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of versioning policy and historical practices

evidence.value: Usage dashboard with metrics

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of available monitoring tools and metrics

evidence.value: Email support, Discord community, comprehensive docs and migration guides

support_quality.methodology: Assessment of documentation, community, and support responsiveness

evidence.value: Available on AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, Azure Foundry; default model in many agent frameworks

ecosystem_maturity.methodology: Analysis of third-party integrations and tools

evidence.value: Standard commercial terms, enterprise agreements available

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions

operational_excellence.notes: Production-ready with multi-cloud availability. Migration from Sonnet 4.5 requires setting effort explicitly (4.6 defaults to high) and removing assistant prefills.

code-generation.notes: Excellent agentic coding at a fraction of Opus cost. Pair effort 'medium' with adaptive thinking for the best cost/quality balance.

customer-support.notes: The sweet spot for support: fast, empathetic, and cost-effective at scale. Use effort 'low' with thinking disabled for high-volume tiers.

content-creation.notes: Strong long-form and marketing content with fast turnaround. Opus tier still leads on the most nuanced pieces.

data-analysis.notes: Solid analytical capabilities with 1M context for large datasets at workhorse pricing.

research-assistant.notes: 1M context handles large corpora; adaptive thinking deepens analysis when needed. Opus preferred for the hardest synthesis tasks.

legal-compliance.notes: Strong privacy posture, HIPAA eligible, 1M context for contract repositories. Escalate the highest-stakes reviews to Opus.

healthcare.notes: HIPAA eligible with strong privacy controls. Well-suited to clinical documentation at production volume.

financial-analysis.notes: Good quantitative reasoning with predictable cost. Use effort 'high' for complex modeling; Opus for the hardest problems.

education.notes: Fast, patient explanations at a price point that scales to large student populations.

creative-writing.notes: Capable creative writing with good narrative flow; Opus tier produces more distinctive prose.

strengths: Best speed/intelligence balance in the Claude lineup at $3/$15 per 1M tokens
1M token context window with 64K max output
Adaptive thinking supported — no manual thinking budgets to tune
Effort parameter including 'max' (not available on Sonnet 4.5 or Haiku)
Strong computer-use accuracy for agentic automation
HIPAA eligible with ephemeral data handling
limitations: Lower ceiling than Opus tier on the hardest reasoning and long-horizon agentic tasks
Removed assistant prefills — code relying on prefills returns 400
Effort defaults to high — Sonnet 4.5 migrations see higher latency/cost unless effort is set explicitly
best_for: Production agentic workloads needing strong quality at predictable cost
High-volume customer support and conversational applications
Agentic coding and tool-heavy workflows with fast turnaround
Long-context processing of large document sets on a budget
not_recommended_for: Frontier-difficulty reasoning where Opus 4.8 is warranted
Workflows still dependent on assistant-turn prefills
Outputs beyond 64K tokens in a single response

pricing.notes: Same pricing as Sonnet 4.5. Batch API 50% discount. Prompt caching up to 90% savings.

metadata.architecture: Transformer-based with Constitutional AI alignment, adaptive thinking, and effort parameter
